Datetime.description: Lifetime during which the address is preferred for use.

InterfaceIndex.description:
Gets the user-friendly index of the server interface.

It's the unique interface index number used by the network stack.

InterfaceAlias.description: Gets the user-friendly name of the server interface.

AddressFamily.description:
Gets the address family of the server address.

Possible values:
- 2  - IPv4
- 23 - IPv6

ServerAddresses.description: Gets a list that contains the DNS server addresses.

DeviceID.description: Uniquely identifies the network adapter on the system.

PnPDeviceID.description: Plug and Play (PnP) device identifier assigned to the adapter by Windows.

InterfaceIndex.description: Unique interface index number used by the network stack.

InterfaceName.description: Name of the network adapter interface.

InterfaceType.description: Type of interface as defined by the IANA Interface Type registry.

InterfaceDescription.description:
Interface description, also known as ifDesc or display name.

This is a unique name assigned to the network adapter during installation.

InterfaceAlias.description: Friendly alias name assigned to the network interface.

InterfaceOperationalStatus.description:
Current operational status of the network adapter interface.

Possible values:
- Up (1)
- Down (2)
- Testing (3)
- Unknown (4)
- Dormant (5)
- NotPresent (6)
- LowerLayerDown (7)

Virtual.description: Indicates whether this adapter represents a virtual interface.

FullDuplex.description: Indicates whether the adapter supports full-duplex mode.

Hidden.description: Indicates whether the adapter is hidden from the user interface.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the network adapter device.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

LinkLayerAddress.description: Physical MAC address of the network adapter.

LinkSpeed.description: Current link speed as a formatted string.

ReceiveLinkSpeed.description: Raw value of the current receive link speed in bits per second.

TransmitLinkSpeed.description: Raw value of the current transmit link speed in bits per second.

DriverName.description: Name of the network adapter driver.

DriverVersion.description: Version number of the network adapter driver.

DriverDate.description: Date of the currently installed driver.

MtuSize.description: Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) size of the adapter in bytes.

MediaConnectState.description:
Current media connection state of the adapter.

Possible values:
- Unknown (0)
- Connected (1)
- Disconnected (2)

NdisMedium.description:
Network adapter media type.

Numeric equivalent of mediaType.

Possible values:
- 802.3 (0)
- 802.5 (1)
- FDDI (2)
- WAN (3)
- LocalTalk (4)
- DIX (5)
- Raw Arcnet (6)
- 878.2 (7)
- ATM (8)
- Wireless WAN (9)
- IrDA (10)
- BPC (11)
- Connection Oriented WAN (12)
- IP 1394 (13)
- InfiniBand (14)
- Tunnel (15)
- Native 802.11 (16)
- Loopback (17)
- WiMAX (18)
- IP (19)

NdisPhysicalMedium.description:
Physical media type supported by the network adapter.

Numeric equivalent of physicalMediaType.

Possible values:
- Unspecified (0)
- Wireless LAN (1)
- Cable Modem (2)
- Phone Line (3)
- Power Line (4)
- DSL (5)
- Fibre Channel (6)
- IEEE 1394 (7)
- Wireless WAN (8)
- Native 802.11 (9)
- Bluetooth (10)
- InfiniBand (11)
- WiMAX (12)
- Ultra-Wideband (13)
- 802.3 (14)
- 802.5 (15)
- IrDA (16)
- Wired WAN (17)
- Wired Connection Oriented WAN (18)
- Other (19)

MediaType.description: Type of network media currently in use.

InterfaceIndex.description: Interface index of the network interface on which the profile is connected.

InterfaceAlias.description: Name of the network interface on which the profile is connected.

NetworkCategory.description:
Network category of the connected profile.

Possible values:
- Public (0)
- Private (1)
- DomainAuthenticated (2)

DomainAuthenticationKind.description:
Indicates the domain authentication kind associated with the profile.

WARNING: No official Microsoft documentation was found for this field.

IPv4Connectivity.description:
IPv4 connectivity status of the connected profile.

Possible values:
- Disconnected (0)
- NoTraffic (1)
- Subnet (2)
- LocalNetwork (3)
- Internet (4)

IPv6Connectivity.description:
IPv6 connectivity status of the connected profile.

Possible values:
- Disconnected (0)
- NoTraffic (1)
- Subnet (2)
- LocalNetwork (3)
- Internet (4)

InterfaceIndex.description: Index of the network interface associated with this IP configuration.

InterfaceAlias.description: User-friendly name of the network interface.

AddressFamily.description:
Address family of the IP address.

Possible values:
- IPv4 (2)
- IPv6 (23)

IPAddress.description: IP address assigned to the interface.

IPv4Address.description: IPv4 address assigned to the interface, if applicable.

IPv6Address.description: IPv6 address assigned to the interface, if applicable.

Type.description:
Type of IP address.

Possible values:
- Unicast (1)
- Anycast (2)

PrefixOrigin.description:
Source of the prefix for this IP address.

Possible values:
- Other (0)
- Manual (1)
- WellKnown (2)
- DHCP (3)
- RouterAdvertisement (4)

SuffixOrigin.description:
Source of the suffix for this IP address.

Possible values:
- Other (0)
- Manual (1)
- WellKnown (2)
- DHCP (3)
- Link (4)
- Random (5)

PrefixLength.description: Length of the network prefix in bits.

PreferredLifetime.description: Lifetime during which the address is preferred for use.

ValidLifetime.description: Total lifetime during which the address is valid.

ReleaseDate.description: BIOS release date in UTC format (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.MMMMMM±OOO).

SMBIOSPresent.description: If true, the SMBIOS is available on this computer system.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the BIOS.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

Version.description: Version of the BIOS. This string is created by the BIOS manufacturer.

CurrentLanguage.description: Name of the current BIOS language.

SMBIOSBIOSVersion.description: BIOS version as reported by SMBIOS.

PrimaryBIOS.description: If TRUE, this is the primary BIOS of the computer system.

Manufacturer.description: Name of the organization responsible for producing the baseboard.

Model.description: Name by which the baseboard is known.

Product.description: Baseboard part number defined by the manufacturer.

SerialNumber.description: Manufacturer-allocated number used to identify the baseboard.

Caption.description: Short, one-line description of the battery object.

Name.description: Label by which the battery is known.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the battery device.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

PowerManagementCapabilities.description:
Array of specific power-related capabilities supported by the battery.

Possible values:
- 0 - Unknown
- 1 - Not Supported
- 2 - Disabled
- 3 - Enabled
- 4 - Power Saving Modes Entered Automatically
- 5 - Power State Settable
- 6 - Power Cycling Supported
- 7 - Timed Power On Supported

PowerManagementSupported.description: Indicates whether the battery can be power-managed.

BatteryStatus.description:
Status of the battery.

Possible values:
- 1 - Discharging
- 2 - AC present, not charging
- 3 - Fully Charged
- 4 - Low
- 5 - Critical
- 6 - Charging
- 7 - Charging and High
- 8 - Charging and Low
- 9 - Charging and Critical
- 10 - Undefined
- 11 - Partially Charged

Chemistry.description:
Type of battery chemistry.

Possible values:
- 1 - Other
- 2 - Unknown
- 3 - Lead Acid
- 4 - Nickel Cadmium
- 5 - Nickel Metal Hydride
- 6 - Lithium-ion
- 7 - Zinc Air
- 8 - Lithium Polymer

DesignCapacity.description: Design capacity of the battery in milliwatt-hours.

DesignVoltage.description: Design voltage of the battery in millivolts.

EstimatedChargeRemaining.description: Estimated percentage of full charge remaining.

EstimatedRunTime.description: Estimated remaining runtime of the battery in minutes.

DeviceID.description: Unique identifier of the cache instance.

Purpose.description: Free-form description of the cache purpose or level designation.

CacheType.description:
Type of cache.

Possible values:
- 1 – Other
- 2 – Unknown
- 3 – Instruction
- 4 – Data
- 5 – Unified

Level.description:
Cache hierarchy level.

Possible values:
- 1 – Other
- 2 – Unknown
- 3 – Primary (L1)
- 4 – Secondary (L2)
- 5 – Tertiary (L3)
- 6 – Not Applicable

Associativity.description:
Cache associativity.

Possible values:
- 1 – Other
- 2 – Unknown
- 3 – Direct Mapped
- 4 – 2-way Set-Associative
- 5 – 4-way Set-Associative
- 6 – Fully Associative
- 7 – 8-way Set-Associative
- 8 – 16-way Set-Associative

Location.description:
Physical cache location relative to the processor.

Possible values:
- 0 – Internal
- 1 – External
- 2 – Reserved
- 3 – Unknown

ErrorCorrectType.description:
Error-correction method used by the cache.

Possible values:
- 0 – Reserved
- 1 – Other
- 2 – Unknown
- 3 – None
- 4 – Parity
- 5 – Single-bit ECC
- 6 – Multi-bit ECC

Availability.description:
Current availability and operational state.

Possible values:
- 1 – Other
- 2 – Unknown
- 3 – Running/Full Power
- 4 – Warning
- 5 – In Test
- 6 – Not Applicable
- 7 – Power Off
- 8 – Offline
- 9 – Off-duty
- 10 – Degraded
- 11 – Not Installed
- 12 – Install Error
- 13 – Power Save - Unknown
- 14 – Power Save - Low Power Mode
- 15 – Power Save - Standby
- 16 – Power Cycle
- 17 – Power Save - Warning
- 18 – Paused
- 19 – Not Ready
- 20 – Not Configured
- 21 – Quiesced

Status.description:
Current operational status of the cache device.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

StatusInfo.description:
Logical state of the device.

Possible values:
- 1 – Other
- 2 – Unknown
- 3 – Enabled
- 4 – Disabled
- 5 – Not Applicable

AdminPasswordStatus.description:
System hardware security settings for administrator password status.

Possible values:
- 0 — Disabled
- 1 — Enabled
- 2 — Not Implemented
- 3 — Unknown

KeyboardPasswordStatus.description:
System hardware security settings for keyboard password status.

Possible values:
- 0 — Disabled
- 1 — Enabled
- 2 — Not Implemented
- 3 — Unknown

PowerOnPasswordStatus.description:
System hardware security settings for power-on password status.

Possible values:
- 0 — Disabled
- 1 — Enabled
- 2 — Not Implemented
- 3 — Unknown

BootupState.description:
System start type.

Possible values:
- "Normal boot" — Normal boot
- "Fail-safe boot" — Safe mode (no network)
- "Fail-safe with network boot" — Safe mode with networking

BootStatus.description:
Status and additional data fields that identify the boot status.
This value comes from the Boot Status member of the System Boot Information structure in the SMBIOS information.

Note: BootStatus is platform/firmware dependent and is not supported before Windows 10 and Windows Server 2016

AutomaticResetBootOption.description: If true, the automatic reset boot option is enabled.

PowerState.description:
Current power state of the computer system.

Possible values:
- 0 — Unknown
- 1 — Full Power
- 2 — Power Save - Low Power Mode
- 3 — Power Save - Standby
- 4 — Power Save - Unknown
- 5 — Power Cycle
- 6 — Power Off
- 7 — Power Save - Warning
- 8 — Power Save - Hibernate
- 9 — Power Save - Soft Off

PowerSupplyState.description:
State of the power supply or supplies when last booted.

Possible values:
- 1 — Other
- 2 — Unknown
- 3 — Safe
- 4 — Warning
- 5 — Critical
- 6 — Non-recoverable

PowerManagementCapabilities.description:
Array of specific power-related capabilities.

Possible values:
- 0 — Unknown
- 1 — Not Supported
- 2 — Disabled
- 3 — Enabled
- 4 — Power Saving Modes Entered Automatically
- 5 — Power State Settable
- 6 — Power Cycling Supported
- 7 — Timed Power On Supported

PowerManagementSupported.description: If true, the device can be power-managed.

ResetCapability.description:
If enabled, indicates the unitary computer system can be reset using power and reset buttons.

Typical values:
- 1 — Other
- 2 — Unknown
- 3 — Disabled
- 4 — Enabled
- 5 — Not Implemented

ResetCount.description:
Number of automatic resets since the last reset.
A value of -1 indicates the count is unknown.

ResetLimit.description:
Number of consecutive times a system reset is attempted.
A value of -1 indicates the limit is unknown.

FrontPanelResetStatus.description:
Hardware security setting for the front-panel reset button.

Typical values:
- 0 — Disabled
- 1 — Enabled
- 2 — Not Implemented
- 3 — Unknown

AutomaticResetCapability.description: If true, automatic reset capability is available.

Name.description: Key of a CIM_System instance. Name of the computer system.

Caption.description: Short one-line description of the object.

Model.description: Product name assigned by the manufacturer.

PrimaryOwnerContact.description: Contact information for the primary owner.

Roles.description: List of roles the system performs in the environment (editable).

ChassisSKUNumber.description: Chassis or enclosure SKU number (from SMBIOS).

SystemSKUNumber.description: SKU/Product ID for the system configuration.

SystemFamily.description: Family of the computer (SMBIOS Family field). May be unsupported on older OS versions.

SystemType.description:
System architecture description

Possible values:
- "x64-based PC"
- "X86-based PC"
- "MIPS-based PC"
- "Alpha-based PC"
- "Power PC"
- "SH-x PC"
- "StrongARM PC"
- "64-bit Intel PC"
- "64-bit Alpha PC"
- "Unknown"
- "X86-Nec98 PC"

UserName.description: Currently logged-on user. In Terminal Services scenarios, this is the console user.

Workgroup.description: Name of the workgroup or domain (if PartOfDomain==false this is a workgroup name).

NumberOfLogicalProcessors.description: Number of logical processors available (includes hyperthreading logical CPUs).

TotalPhysicalMemory.description:
Total size of physical memory in bytes.
Note: under some circumstances this may not be accurate (BIOS reservation). For accurate module-by-module capacity,
query the equivalent method(s) in Win32_PhysicalMemory

AutomaticManagedPagefile.description: If true, the system manages the page file automatically.

InfraredSupported.description: If true, an infrared (IR) port exists on the computer system.

NetworkServerModeEnabled.description: If true, network server mode is enabled (system behaves as a server).

HypervisorPresent.description:
If true, a hypervisor is present on the system.
Note: not supported before Windows 8 / Windows Server 2012 on older OSes.

ThermalState.description:
Thermal state of the system when last booted.

Possible values:
- 1 — Other
- 2 — Unknown
- 3 — Safe
- 4 — Warning
- 5 — Critical
- 6 — Non-recoverable

CurrentTimeZone.description:
Amount of time the system is offset from UTC, in minutes.
Example: for UTC+5:30 (Asia/Kolkata) the value is 330.

DaylightInEffect.description: If True, the daylight savings mode is ON.

DeviceID.description: Unique identifier of the desktop monitor on the system.

Name.description: Label by which the object is known.

PNPDeviceID.description: Windows Plug and Play device identifier of the monitor.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the monitor device.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

MonitorManufacturer.description: Name of the manufacturer of the monitor.

PixelsPerXLogicalInch.description: Resolution along the x-axis (horizontal direction) of the monitor.

PixelsPerYLogicalInch.description: Resolution along the y-axis (vertical direction) of the monitor.

DeviceID.description: Unique identifier of the disk drive with other devices on the system.

Model.description: Manufacturer’s model number of the disk drive.

Size.description:
Size of the disk drive, calculated by multiplying the total number
of cylinders, tracks in each cylinder, sectors in each track,
and bytes in each sector.

FirmwareRevision.description: Revision of the disk drive firmware assigned by the manufacturer.

SerialNumber.description: Number allocated by the manufacturer to identify the physical media.

Partitions.description: Number of partitions on this physical disk drive recognized by the operating system.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the physical disk.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

InterfaceType.description: Interface type of the physical disk drive (e.g., SCSI, IDE, USB, NVMe).

PNPDeviceID.description: Windows Plug and Play device identifier of the logical device.

Capabilities.description:
Array of capabilities of the media access device.

Possible Values:
- 1 - Other
- 2 - Sequential Access
- 3 - Random Access
- 4 - Supports Writing
- 5 - Encryption
- 6 - Compression
- 7 - Supports Removable Media
- 8 - Manual Cleaning
- 9 - Automatic Cleaning
- 10 - S.M.A.R.T Notification
- 11 - Supports Dual Sided Media
- 12 - Pre-dismount Eject Not Required

CapabilityDescriptions.description:
List of more detailed explanations for any of the access device
features indicated in the capabilities array.

Each entry of this array is related to the entry in the
capabilities array located at the same index.

DeviceID.description: Unique identifier of the disk drive and partition within the system.

Name.description: Label by which the partition is known.

BlockSize.description: Size in bytes of the blocks that form this partition.

NumberOfBlocks.description:
Total number of consecutive blocks that form this partition.

The total size of the partition can be calculated by
multiplying this value by blockSize.

Bootable.description: Indicates whether the computer can be booted from this partition.

PrimaryPartition.description: Indicates whether this is the primary partition on the disk.

BootPartition.description:
Indicates whether this is the active (boot) partition
used by the operating system when booting.

DiskIndex.description: Index number of the physical disk that contains this partition.

Size.description: Total size of the partition in bytes.

Type.description:
Type of the partition.

Possible Values (Non-exhaustive, will be updated when new values are found):
- Unused
- 12-bit FAT
- Xenix Type 1
- Xenix Type 2
- 16-bit FAT
- Extended Partition
- MS-DOS V4 Huge
- Installable File System
- PowerPC Reference Platform
- UNIX
- NTFS
- ReFS
- Win95 w/Extended Int 13
- Extended w/Extended Int 13
- Logical Disk Manager
- Unknown

Name.description: Character string that specifies the name of a Windows-based environment variable.

SystemVariable.description: Indicates whether the variable is a system variable.

VariableValue.description:
Placeholder variable of a Windows-based environment variable.
Information such as file system directories can change from computer to computer.
The operating system substitutes placeholders for these values.

DeviceID.description:
Unique identifier of the logical disk from other devices on the system.
Appears as the drive letter assigned to the partition in the physical disk
Example: "C:"

Description.description: Description of the logical disk object.

DriveType.description:
Numeric value that corresponds to the type of disk drive this logical disk represents.

Possible values:
- 0 – Unknown
- 1 – No Root Directory
- 2 – Removable Disk
- 3 – Local Disk
- 4 – Network Drive
- 5 – Compact Disc
- 6 – RAM Disk

Data type: uint32

MediaType.description:
Type of media currently present in the logical drive.
Value corresponds to a member of the MEDIA_TYPE enumeration defined in Winioctl.h.
Visit the microsoft documentation stated at the class level to know about the possible values.

FileSystem.description:
File system on the logical disk.
Example: "NTFS", "FAT32", "ReFS"

Size.description: Size of the disk drive in bytes.

FreeSpace.description: Free space, in bytes, available on the logical disk.

Compressed.description:
Indicates if the logical volume exists as a single compressed entity (e.g., DoubleSpace).
If file-based compression is supported (e.g., NTFS), this value is false.

SupportsFileBasedCompression.description: Indicates whether the logical disk supports file-based compression (e.g., NTFS).

SupportsDiskQuotas.description: Indicates whether this volume supports disk quotas.

VolumeName.description:
Volume name of the logical disk.
Example: "Local Disk"

VolumeSerialNumber.description: Volume serial number of the logical disk.

Caption.description: Short textual description (friendly name) of the operating system.

InstallDate.description: Date and time when the operating system was installed.

CSName.description: Name of the computer system running the operating system.

LastBootUpTime.description: Date and time when the computer was last booted.

LocalDateTime.description: Current local date and time of the operating system.

Distributed.description: Indicates whether this operating system is part of a distributed system.

NumberOfUsers.description: Number of user sessions currently active.

Version.description:
Version number of the operating system.
Example: "10.0.22621"

BootDevice.description: Path to the boot device that the operating system uses to start the computer.

BuildNumber.description: Internal build number of the operating system.

BuildType.description:
Type of build of the operating system.
Example: "Multiprocessor Free", "Checked"

Manufacturer.description:
Manufacturer of the operating system.
Typically: "Microsoft Corporation"

OSArchitecture.description:
Architecture of the operating system.
Example: "32-bit", "64-bit"

MUILanguages.description:
List of installed user interface languages (MUI language codes).
Example: ["en-US", "fr-FR"]

PortableOperatingSystem.description:
Indicates whether the operating system is installed on a portable device.

Possible values:
- true – Installed on a portable device (e.g., Windows To Go)
- false – Installed on a fixed computer

Primary.description: Indicates whether this is the primary operating system on the computer.

RegisteredUser.description: Name of the registered user of the operating system.

SerialNumber.description: Operating system serial number or product key identifier.

ServicePackMajorVersion.description: Major version number of the most recent service pack installed.

ServicePackMinorVersion.description: Minor version number of the most recent service pack installed.

SystemDirectory.description:
Full path to the system directory.
Example: "C:\WINDOWS\system32"

SystemDrive.description:
Drive letter where the operating system is installed.
Example: "C:"

WindowsDirectory.description:
Full path to the Windows installation directory.
Example: "C:\WINDOWS"

Tag.description: Unique identifier for the physical memory device represented by an instance of this class.

Manufacturer.description: Name of the organization responsible for producing the physical memory.

Model.description: Model name for the physical element.

OtherIdentifyingInfo.description:
Additional data, beyond asset tag information, that can be used to identify
a physical element.

For example, bar code data associated with an element that also has an asset tag.

PartNumber.description:
Part number assigned by the organization responsible for producing
or manufacturing the physical element.

FormFactor.description:
Implementation form factor for the chip.

Possible values:
- 0 - Unknown
- 1 - Other
- 2 - SIP
- 3 - DIP
- 4 - ZIP
- 5 - SOJ
- 6 - Proprietary
- 7 - SIMM
- 8 - DIMM
- 9 - TSOP
- 10 - PGA
- 11 - RIMM
- 12 - SODIMM
- 13 - SRIMM
- 14 - SMD
- 15 - SSMP
- 16 - QFP
- 17 - TQFP
- 18 - SOIC
- 19 - LCC
- 20 - PLCC
- 21 - BGA
- 22 - FPBGA
- 23 - LGA

BankLabel.description: Physically labeled bank where the memory is located.

Capacity.description: Total capacity of the physical memory in bytes.

DataWidth.description:
Data width of the physical memory in bits.

A data width of 0 (zero) and a total width of 8 (eight)
indicates that the memory is used solely to provide error correction bits.

Speed.description: Speed of the physical memory in MHz.

ConfiguredClockSpeed.description:
The configured clock speed of the memory device, in MHz,
or 0 if the speed is unknown.

DeviceLocator.description: Label of the socket or circuit board that holds the memory.

SerialNumber.description: Manufacturer-allocated number to identify the physical element.

DeviceID.description: Identifier of the Plug and Play device.

PNPDeviceID.description: Windows Plug and Play device identifier of the logical device.

HardwareID.description:
Vendor-defined list of hardware identification strings used by Windows Setup
to match the device to an INF file.

CompatibleID.description:
Vendor-defined list of compatible identification strings used by Windows Setup
as fallback identifiers when no matching hardware ID is found.

Name.description: The name by which the device is known.

Manufacturer.description: Name of the manufacturer of the Plug and Play device.

Present.description:
Indicates whether this Plug and Play device is currently present in the system.

Note:
This property is not supported on:
- Windows Server 2012 R2
- Windows 8.1
- Windows Server 2012
- Windows 8
- Windows Server 2008 R2
- Windows 7
- Windows Server 2008
- Windows Vista

Status.description:
Current operational status of the Plug and Play device.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- "OK"
- "Degraded"
- "Pred Fail"

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- "Unknown"
- "Error"
- "Starting"
- "Stopping"
- "Service"

Possible OTHER values:
- "Stressed"
- "NonRecover"
- "No Contact"
- "Lost Comm"

Tag.description: Unique identifier of a port connection on the computer system.

ExternalReferenceDesignator.description:
External reference designator of the port.

External reference designators are identifiers that determine
the type and use of the port.

InternalReferenceDesignator.description:
Internal reference designator of the port.

Internal reference designators are specific to the manufacturer,
and identify the circuit board location or use of the port.

PortType.description:
Function of the port.

Possible values include:
- None (0)
- Parallel Port XT/AT Compatible (1)
- Parallel Port PS/2 (2)
- Parallel Port ECP (3)
- Parallel Port EPP (4)
- Parallel Port ECP/EPP (5)
- Serial Port XT/AT Compatible (6)
- Serial Port 16450 Compatible (7)
- Serial Port 16550 Compatible (8)
- Serial Port 16550A Compatible (9)
- SCSI Port (10)
- MIDI Port (11)
- Joy Stick Port (12)
- Keyboard Port (13)
- Mouse Port (14)
- SSA SCSI (15)
- USB (16)
- FireWire (IEEE P1394) (17)
- PCMCIA Type I (18)
- PCMCIA Type II (19)
- PCMCIA Type III (20)
- Cardbus (21)
- Access Bus Port (22)
- SCSI II (23)
- SCSI Wide (24)
- PC-98 (25)
- PC-98-Hireso (26)
- PC-H98 (27)
- Video Port (28)
- Audio Port (29)
- Modem Port (30)
- Network Port (31)
- 8251 Compatible (32)
- 8251 FIFO Compatible (33)

ConnectorType.description:
Array of physical attributes of the connector used by this port.

Refer to the Microsoft documentation provided at the class level
for a list of possible values.

DeviceID.description: System-assigned unique identifier of the printer.

Name.description: Name of the printer as recognized by the system.

PNPDeviceID.description: Windows Plug and Play device identifier.

Capabilities.description:
List of capability codes supported by the printer.

Possible values:
- 1 - Other
- 2 - Color printing
- 3 - Duplex printing
- 4 - Copies
- 5 - Collation
- 6 - Stapling
- 7 - Transparency printing
- 8 - Punch
- 9 - Cover
- 10 - Bind
- 11 - Black-and-white printing
- 12 - One-sided
- 13 - Two-sided long edge
- 14 - Two-sided short edge
- 15 - Portrait
- 16 - Landscape
- 17 - Reverse Portrait
- 18 - Reverse Landscape
- 19 - Quality High
- 20 - Quality Normal
- 21 - Quality Low

HorizontalResolution.description: Printer horizontal resolution in DPI (dots per inch).

VerticalResolution.description: Printer vertical resolution in DPI (dots per inch).

PaperSizesSupported.description:
Numeric codes of paper sizes supported by the printer.

Refer to the Microsoft documentation for the exhaustive list of supported values.

PrinterPaperNames.description: Names of paper types or forms supported by the printer.

PrinterStatus.description:
Current operational state of the printer.

Possible values:
- 1 - Other
- 2 - Unknown
- 3 - Idle
- 4 - Printing
- 5 - Warm-up
- 6 - Stopped printing
- 7 - Offline

PrintJobDataType.description:
Data type of print jobs.

Example: RAW or EMF

PrintProcessor.description:
Print processor used to process print jobs.

Example: WinPrint

DriverName.description: Name of the printer driver installed.

Shared.description: Indicates whether the printer is shared on the network.

ShareName.description: Share name of the printer if it is shared.

SpoolEnabled.description: Indicates whether spooling is enabled for the printer.

Hidden.description: Specifies whether the printer is hidden from standard user interfaces.

SessionId.description: Identifier of the session under which this process is running.

Name.description: Name of the executable file responsible for this process.

Caption.description: Short one-line description of the process.

ExecutablePath.description: Full path to the executable file of the process.

ExecutionState.description:
Current execution state of the process.

Possible values:
- 0 — Unknown
- 1 — Other
- 2 — Ready
- 3 — Running
- 4 — Blocked
- 5 — Suspended Blocked
- 6 — Suspended Ready
- 7 — Terminated
- 8 — Stopped
- 9 — Growing

Handle.description: Handle of the process (string representation of ProcessId).

HandleCount.description: Number of handles currently open by the process.

Priority.description:
Scheduling priority of the process.

Possible values:
- 0 (lowest) to 31 (highest)

ThreadCount.description: Number of active threads in the process.

KernelModeTime.description: Time spent by the process in kernel mode, in milliseconds.

UserModeTime.description: Time spent by the process in user mode, in milliseconds.

WorkingSetSize.description: Current working set size used by the process, in bytes.

PeakWorkingSetSize.description: Peak working set size of the process, in kilobytes.

PrivatePageCount.description: Current number of private memory pages used by the process.

PageFileUsage.description: Current amount of page file usage, in kilobytes.

PeakPageFileUsage.description: Peak page file usage, in kilobytes.

VirtualSize.description: Current virtual address space used by the process, in bytes.

PeakVirtualSize.description: Peak virtual address space used by the process, in bytes.

CreationDate.description: Date and time when the process was created.

TerminationDate.description: Date and time when the process was terminated, if available.

DeviceID.description: Unique identifier of the processor on the system.

Name.description: Processor name. Typically includes manufacturer, brand, and model information.

NumberOfCores.description: Number of physical cores on the processor.

ThreadCount.description: Number of hardware threads across all cores.

NumberOfLogicalProcessors.description: Number of logical processors on the system.

AddressWidth.description:
Width of the processor address bus in bits.

For a 32-bit CPU the value is 32 and for a 64-bit CPU,
the value is 64.

L2CacheSize.description: Size of the Level 2 cache in kilobytes.

L3CacheSize.description: Size of the Level 3 cache in kilobytes.

MaxClockSpeed.description:
Maximum speed of the processor in megahertz
under normal operating conditions.

ExtClock.description: External clock frequency of the processor in megahertz.

SocketDesignation.description: Type of socket or slot used by the processor.

Version.description: Version of the processor as reported by the manufacturer.

Caption.description: Short textual description of the processor.

Family.description:
Processor family type.

Indicates the manufacturer and generation of the processor.

Stepping.description: Stepping information for the processor revision.

VirtualizationFirmwareEnabled.description: Indicates whether virtualization technology is enabled in firmware.

ProcessorId.description:
Processor identifier string, which may include family,
model, and stepping information.

Architecture.description:
Processor architecture used by the platform.

Possible values:
- x86 (0)
- MIPS (1)
- Alpha (2)
- PowerPC (3)
- ARM (5)
- ia64 (6)
- x64 (9)
- ARM64 (12)

DeviceID.description: Unique identifier of the sound device.

PNPDeviceID.description: Windows Plug and Play device identifier.

Name.description: Friendly name of the sound device as recognized by the system.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the sound device.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- OK
- Degraded
- Pred Fail

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- Unknown
- Error
- Starting
- Stopping
- Service

Possible OTHER values:
- Stressed
- NonRecover
- No Contact
- Lost Comm

StatusInfo.description:
Numeric state of the logical device.

Possible values:
- 1 - Other
- 2 - Unknown
- 3 - Enabled
- 4 - Disabled
- 5 - Not Applicable

SID.description:
Security identifier (SID) for this account.

A SID is a string value of variable length that is used to identify a trustee.
Each account has a unique SID that an authority, such as a Windows domain, issues.
The SID is stored in the security database.
When a user logs on, the system retrieves the user SID from the database,
places the SID in the user access token,
and then uses the SID in the user access token to identify the user
in all subsequent interactions with Windows security.
Each SID is a unique identifier for a user or group, and a different user or group cannot have the same SID.

SIDType.description:
Type of SID.

Possible values:
- 1 — User
- 2 — Group
- 3 — Domain
- 4 — Alias
- 5 — WellKnownGroup
- 6 — DeletedAccount
- 7 — Invalid
- 8 — Unknown
- 9 — Computer

AccountType.description:
Type of user account.

Possible values:
- 256 — Temporary duplicate account
- 512 — Normal account
- 2048 — Interdomain trust account
- 4096 — Workstation trust account
- 8192 — Server trust account

Caption.description: Caption of the user account in the format domain/username.

Domain.description: Domain to which the user account belongs.

Disabled.description: Indicates whether the account is disabled.

LocalAccount.description: Indicates whether this is a local account.

Lockout.description: Indicates whether the account is locked out.

PasswordRequired.description: Indicates whether a password is required for the account.

PasswordChangeable.description: Indicates whether the password can be changed.

Status.description:
Current operational status of the account.

Possible OPERATIONAL values:
- "OK"
- "Degraded"
- "Pred Fail"

Possible NON-OPERATIONAL values:
- "Unknown"
- "Error"
- "Starting"
- "Stopping"
- "Service"

Possible OTHER values:
- "Stressed"
- "NonRecover"
- "No Contact"
- "Lost Comm"

DeviceID.description: Identifier (unique to the computer system) for this video controller.

Name.description: Label by which the video controller is known.

PNPDeviceID.description: Windows Plug and Play device identifier of the video controller.

CurrentBitsPerPixel.description: Number of bits used to display each pixel.

CurrentRefreshRate.description:
Frequency at which the video controller refreshes the image for the monitor.

Unit: hertz (Hz).

MaxRefreshRate.description: Maximum refresh rate of the video controller in hertz.

MinRefreshRate.description: Minimum refresh rate of the video controller in hertz.

AdapterDACType.description:
Name or identifier of the digital-to-analog converter (DAC) chip.

The character set of this property is alphanumeric.

AdapterRAM.description: Memory size of the video adapter in bytes.

DriverDate.description: Last modification date and time of the currently installed video driver.

DriverVersion.description: Version number of the video driver.

VideoProcessor.description: Free-form string describing the video processor.

info.title: Cimari REST
info.description: Transform cimari codebase into a REST Service
